summary: Joel Spolsky shares a New York Times feature covering Fog Creek Software's new office space. The brief post highlights the NYT's recognition that Spolsky, despite being a software designer rather than an architect, demonstrated genuine design knowledge in creating the office. Spolsky links to additional resources including his own detailed write-up and a 49-picture slideshow for readers wanting more details about the space.
key_points: The New York Times featured Fog Creek's new office in their commercial real estate section
The NYT noted that Spolsky's design knowledge exceeded expectations for a software industry client
Spolsky had previously documented the office design process in detail on his blog
money_quotes: A client who claims to know something about design might be an architect's worst nightmare. But it turns out that Joel Spolsky, a software designer, author and blogger, actually knows a lot about it.
key_insight: Fog Creek's investment in thoughtful office design earned mainstream media recognition, reflecting Spolsky's belief that workspace quality matters for software companies.
